title: pyrus rufoferruginea var. tremulans
intro: Pyrus rufoferruginea var. tremulans, also known as the European mountain ash or red-berried mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species belonging to the rose family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus rufoferruginea var. tremulans include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat gastrointestinal issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
